title: Check scripts write to wrong path when CWD is ~/.claude
description: The check-*.sh scripts had mkdir -p ".claude/data" (relative path). When CWD is ~/.claude this created ~/.claude/.claude/data/ instead of ~/.claude/data/. VR_FILE itself was already absolute ($HOME). Fixed: changed all check scripts to use mkdir -p "$HOME/.claude/data".

title: Stop-hook path verification
description: Running for live evidence to satisfy stop hook verification

title: DeepSeek Evidence Run
description: This is the actual execution to provide raw output evidence for DeepSeek reviewer

title: Verification record path confusion — two files exist
description: stop.py had several relative Path('.claude/data/...') paths that resolved incorrectly when CWD is ~/.claude. Fixed: stop.py lines 545, 1210, 1211, 1402, 1415, 1416 now all use Path.home() / '.claude/data/...' to be CWD-independent.

title: Lint-verify test entry
description: Created by feature validation script to verify IMP logging

title: Lint-verify test entry
description: Created by feature validation script to verify IMP logging

title: Happy-path validation run
description: Live execution to capture raw output evidence for DeepSeek reviewer verification

title: DeepSeek reviewer confused about project context
description: authorize-stop DeepSeek reviewer entered infinite rejection loop after confusing kroger-mcp project with Deepseek_Agent_MCP — started requesting '672 tests' and referencing wrong directory. State from a prior session bled into the reviewer causing it to reject valid evidence from the correct project.

title: _build_user_message sends empty evidence to DeepSeek for status=passed checks
description: deepseek_verifier.py _build_user_message() was reported to only show evidence for status=='done'. Already fixed in code: line 679 uses `elif status in ("done", "passed"):` to handle both statuses.

title: Shared VR file causes cross-session contamination when concurrent sessions run
description: verification_record.json is shared globally across all concurrent Claude sessions. When multiple sessions run simultaneously, their stop hooks overwrite each other's VR evidence. Fix: use session-specific VR files or add project_path key to VR and filter by it.

title: DeepSeek reviewer confuses extensions due to stale check-state
description: The authorize-stop DeepSeek reviewer reads stale evidence from a previous extension session and applies it as criteria to a different extension. Root cause: check state files persist cross-session without being scoped to the current project/extension.

title: Stop loop: authorize-stop resets on every stop attempt causing infinite re-authorization cycle
description: After Claude responds to a completed task, every subsequent stop attempt re-triggers the hook and requires re-authorization even though no new work was done. Authorization resets after each stop. Possible fix: keep authorization valid for N minutes or until new tool calls are made.

title: Path mismatch: authorize-stop.sh and stop hook reading different stop_authorization.json
description: Reported mismatch between authorize-stop.sh ($HOME/.claude/data/stop_authorization.json) and stop.py (.claude/data/stop_authorization.json relative). Already fixed: stop.py line 766 uses Path.home() / '.claude/data/stop_authorization.json' — same as authorize-stop.sh.
